snippets.description: H1: httpsUrl() now exported + new validateSentrySnapshot() — rejects non-https permalinks, format-checks issueId (/^\d{1,32}$/) and shortId (/^[A-Za-z0-9._-]{0,64}$/), caps title <=500. Returns null on any failure.
snippets.content: export function validateSentrySnapshot(input): SentrySnapshotInput | null { ... if (permalink !== '' && httpsUrl(permalink) === '') return null; ... }

snippets.description: H1: ?/track calls validateSentrySnapshot; null -> fail(400, 'Invalid Sentry permalink.'). Legit UI always posts https from the Sentry API.
snippets.content: const snapshot = validateSentrySnapshot({...}); if (!snapshot) return fail(400, { action: 'track', error: 'Invalid Sentry permalink.' });

snippets.description: H2: admin_issue_event_type_ck CHECK relaxed to add 'note_edited'. Migration 0046_oval_lorna_dane.sql generated + DDL applied to local dev DB (push-managed).

H2: setIssueStatus returns 'updated'|'unchanged'|'not_found'; before-query now selects resolutionNote; same-status resolved+note-differs branch persists the note + records a note_edited event (audit trail), never mutating resolution metadata silently.
snippets.content: if (before.status === status) { if (resolving && newNote !== before.resolutionNote) { ...recordEvent(tx,{type: ISSUE_EVENT.noteEdited, note:newNote}); return 'updated'; } return 'unchanged'; }

snippets.description: M1+L4: innerJoin(assignee) -> leftJoin(assigneeJoinOn) with assignedAt <= event.createdAt bound (M1); audience predicate ORs isNotNull(assignee) with (type='unassigned' AND toValue=userId) so a removed person still sees their own unassignment (L4). Applied to all 3 query sites in one pass.
snippets.content: and(eq(assignee.issueId, event.issueId), eq(assignee.adminUserId, userId), lte(assignee.assignedAt, event.createdAt)) ; or(isNotNull(assignee.adminUserId), and(eq(type,'unassigned'), eq(toValue, userId)))

snippets.description: M3: ISSUE_STATUS added to the existing @veent/core import; isPoolItem now requires status === ISSUE_STATUS.open so a resolved/in-progress unassigned incident is NOT pool-readable by a non-assignee.

M2: both tracked-despite-gitignore secret artifacts staged for removal via git rm --cached (files remain on disk; harness regenerates). RESIDUAL: the leaked session cookie + TOTP secret remain in git history and must be rotated.
